title: Infrastructure Project Manager - M&A
description: Infrastructure Project Manager - M&A Are you early in your career and looking to build deep expertise in infrastructure, insurance, M&A and project finance—while working alongside experienced industry specialists on high‑profile transactions? Are you intellectually curious and able to bring a foundational background from the insurance sector ? If so, we'd love to hear from you in connection with this exciting new hybrid role that comes with the flexibility to work both virtually and from our flagship London office. Aon is in the business of better decisions At Aon, we shape decisions for the better to protect and enrich the lives of people around the world. As an organisation, we are united through trust as one inclusive team and we are passionate about helping our colleagues and clients succeed. What the day will look like Take ownership of end-to-end project and programme management for corporate development initiatives, spanning due diligence through to integration and transition. Act as the central hub for sophisticated M&A activity, coordinating collaborators across corporate development, the business, and external counterparties to keep projects moving smoothly. Lead structured governance and delivery, building robust project plans, aligning schedules, managing risks, and overseeing budgets across multiple workstreams. Drive clear, consistent communication, ensuring all parties are aligned on priorities, milestones, and deliverables throughout the transaction lifecycle. Produce insightful reporting and analysis across individual projects and the wider portfolio, enabling informed decision‑making by senior stakeholders. Champion continuous improvement, capturing lessons learned, strengthening relationships with workstream leads, and feeding improvements back into future deals and processes. How this opportunity is different This isn’t a traditional insurance role. From day one, you’ll work on live, high‑profile infrastructure M&A and project finance transactions, partnering with investors, lenders and corporates across the UK and EMEA. You’ll gain early exposure to complex deals, learn directly from senior specialists across insurance, banking and legal backgrounds, and build deep advisory skills that go far beyond placement or underwriting. It’s a rare opportunity to develop a long‑term career at the heart of one of Aon’s fastest‑growing transaction advisory practices. Skills and experience that will lead to success Experience in the insurance industry (e.g. broking or underwriting property & casualty or specialty lines) Strong organisational and project management skills, with the ability to manage multiple workstreams Clear written and verbal communication skills An analytical approach and enthusiastic appetite to learn and develop your skills Strong interpersonal skills and a collaborative approach Progress toward or completion of relevant insurance qualifications (e.g. ACII) Language skills: English (additional foreign language skills beneficial). Comfortable with flexible work schedule. #LI-AB2 How we support our colleagues In addition to our comprehensive benefits package, we encourage an inclusive workforce. Plus, our agile environment allows you to manage your wellbeing and work/life balance, ensuring you can be your best self at Aon. Furthermore, all colleagues enjoy two “Global Wellbeing Days” each year, encouraging you to take time to focus on yourself. We offer a variety of working style solutions for our colleagues as well. Our continuous learning culture inspires and equips you to learn, share and grow, helping you achieve your fullest potential. As a result, at Aon, you are more connected, more relevant, and more valued. Aon values an innovative and inclusive workplace where all colleagues feel empowered to be their authentic selves. Aon is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace. Aon provides equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, creed, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, veteran, marital, domestic partner status, or other legally protected status. We are committed to providing equal employment opportunities and fostering an inclusive workplace. If you require accommodations during the application or interview process, please let us know. You can request accommodations by emailing us at ReasonableAccommodations@Aon.com or your recruiter. We will work with you to meet your needs and ensure a fair and equitable experience. #LI-AB2 2572972

title: Solutions Consultant (Consumer Benefits Solutions)
description: Solutions Consultant (Consumer Benefits Solutions) Are you a Product Subject Matter Expert looking to work alongside other Benefits experts to help identify new client needs? Then consider our Solutions Consultant role working in our Consumer Benefits Solutions team. This is a remote role with the flexibility to work virtually from your home anywhere in the Continental United States. Aon is in the business of better decisions: At Aon, we shape decisions for the better to protect and enrich the lives of people around the world. As an organization, we are united through trust as one inclusive, diverse team, and we are passionate about helping our colleagues and clients succeed. What the day will look like: The Solutions Consultant is responsible for Aon Consumer Benefit Solutions using expertise in product consulting and knowledge on how those products fit on various industry platforms for prospective clients during the sales process as well as existing clients during renewal discussions. The primary focus of the Solutions Consultant is to communicate product suite and enrollment value propositions externally to prospective clients. The Solutions Consultant is responsible for assisting with the sales process from prospect stage through the hand-off to the implementation team and signed contract. In addition, the Solutions Consultant is responsible for coordinating Voluntary Benefits Carrier and Product evaluation and selection. As a key sales stakeholder, they will also collaborate with the Lead Sales Consultants on recommendations and enhancements to help us achieve excellence in sales delivery. Job Responsibilities: Manage multiple projects concurrently Maintains subject matter expertise on Voluntary Benefit products and Voluntary Benefit carriers Maintains expertise in proprietary and 3rd party Benefits Administration systems Articulates and communicates value proposition and key differentiators Develops a high level working knowledge of other practice area solutions Collaborates with Sales Leaders to communicate market knowledge and sales process and to keep abreast of operations Partners with practice consulting arm to strengthen integration Educates Sales Consultants on specific strategies, solutions, and industry changes Maintains Request for Proposal (RFP) Content Partners with internal partners for Request for Proposal (RFP) requests to ensure a streamlined and efficient process Sales Process and Meetings: Assists Sales Leaders in the development of prospect sales strategy Responsible for defining the scope for the engagement Enables and articulates a strong value-based message on any sales opportunities Leads system demonstration in sales meetings Prepares follow up client/TPE/carrier question responses with Sales Leaders Performs due diligence to scope work and provide input to strategic questions required for defining the opportunity. Works with the Sales Leaders to request missing information from the Third Party Evaluator (TPE), client or carriers Reviews discovery document outlining the detailed solution Tailors materials and demo based on solution, client need and competitive landscape Collaborates on Carrier and Product Selection Assists on Contract/Statement of Work creation and execution How this opportunity is different: This is a unique opportunity at Aon to be on the front lines of one of the fastest growing segments within the company. This role is true client consulting to help clients meet unmet and unfulfilled needs with innovative solutions for open enrollment engagement, voluntary benefit product suite and technology. Skills and experience that will lead to success: Three plus years of employee benefits industry experience Three plus years managing project schedules Experience working in a highly collaborative, team-oriented environment Interpersonal skills, to help negotiate priorities and to resolve conflicts among project stakeholders (such as operations, product management, and the development team). Maintain a cordial and effective relationship with clients, co-workers, carriers, vendors and other business contacts. Ability to work in a team environment. Excellent verbal, writing and listening skills and the ability to multitask and organize. Sales and other client engagement experience is a plus Excellent Microsoft office suite skills including Excel, Word and PowerPoint. Microsoft Access is a plus Education: Bachelor’s degree or equivalent years of industry experience. Pay Transparency: The salary range for this position (intended for U.S. applicants) is $100,000-$120,000 The actual salary will vary based on applicant’s education, experience, skills, and abilities, as well as internal equity and alignment with market data. The salary may also be adjusted based on applicant’s geographic location. This position is eligible to participate in one of Aon’s annual incentive plans to receive an annual discretionary bonus in addition to base salary. The amount of any bonus varies and is subject to the terms and conditions of the applicable incentive plan. How we support our colleagues Aon offers a comprehensive package of benefits for full-time and regular part-time colleagues, including, but not limited to: a 401(k) savings plan with employer contributions; an employee stock purchase plan; consideration for long-term incentive awards at Aon’s discretion; medical, dental and vision insurance, various types of leaves of absence, paid time off, including 12 paid holidays throughout the calendar year, 15 days of paid vacation per year, paid sick leave as provided under state and local paid sick leave laws, short-term disability and optional long-term disability, health savings account, health care and dependent care reimbursement accounts, employee and dependent life insurance and supplemental life and AD&D insurance; optional personal insurance policies, adoption assistance, tuition assistance, commuter benefits, and an employee assistance program that includes free counseling sessions. Eligibility for benefits is governed by the applicable plan documents and policies. In addition to our comprehensive benefits package, we encourage an inclusive workforce. Plus, our agile environment allows you to manage your wellbeing and work/life balance, ensuring you can be your best self at Aon. Furthermore, all colleagues enjoy two “Global Wellbeing Days” each year, encouraging you to take time to focus on yourself. We offer a variety of working style solutions for our colleagues as well. Our continuous learning culture inspires and equips you to learn, share and grow, helping you achieve your fullest potential. As a result, at Aon, you are more connected, more relevant, and more valued. Aon values an innovative and inclusive workplace where all colleagues feel empowered to be their authentic selves. Aon is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace. Aon provides equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, creed, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, veteran, marital, domestic partner status, or other legally protected status. People with criminal histories are encouraged to apply. We welcome applications from all and provide individuals with disabilities with reasonable adjustments to participate in the job application, interview process and to perform essential job functions once onboard. If you would like to learn more about the reasonable accommodations we provide, email ReasonableAccommodations@Aon.com For positions in San Francisco and Los Angeles, we will consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest and conviction record in accordance with local Fair Chance ordinances. Aon is not accepting unsolicited resumes from search firms for this position. If you are a search firm, you will not be compensated in any way for your submission of a candidate, even if Aon hires that candidate. Nothing in this job description restricts management's right to assign or reassign duties and responsibilities to this job at any time. #LI-DD1 #LI-HYBRID 2577754

title: IND Analyst II - Content Management
description: Job Title- IND Analyst II GSMS-Content Mgmt ARL- 8.2 Solution Line- GSMS Position type- Full Time Work Location- Bangalore, Whitefield Working style- In Office Cab Facility- Yes Shift Time -12.30 p.m. to 9.30 p.m. People Manager role: No Required education and certifications critical for the role- Any Graduate or Post-Graduate (full time) Required years of experience – 2-3 year of relevant experience AON IS IN THE BUSINESS OF BETTER DECISIONS At Aon, we shape decisions for the better to protect and enrich the lives of people around the world. As an organization, we are committed as one firm to our purpose, united through trust as one inclusive, diverse team and we are passionate about helping our colleagues and clients succeed. INFORMATION ABOUT THE BUSINESS Global Sales and Marketing Support team supports Aon business units across geographies with research, analytics, marketing, proposal support and operational support to client delivery. Its mission is to provide Aon sales, marketing and strategy teams with the information, analysis and insight necessary to make informed decisions through business development, client delivery and strategy GENERAL DESCRIPTION OF ROLE: Incumbent would be responsible Manage the sales content knowledge sharing workflow to ensure that content is centralized and shared in a brand compliant format enabling colleagues to bring the best of Aon to their clients. JOB RESPONSIBILITIES: 2-3 years’ experience is desirable. Keep content up to date, relevant and complete, liaising with stakeholders around the business and archiving old content. Help develop the system and content governance approach Previous content management experience or knowledge sharing platform desirable Manage the sales content knowledge sharing workflow to ensure that content is centralized and shared in a brand compliant format enabling colleagues to bring the best of Aon to their clients. Keep content up to date, relevant and complete, liaising with stakeholders around the business and archiving old content. The content/knowledge manager is responsible for overseeing all knowledge-related activities, including the management, capturing, sharing and accessibility of content/knowledge assets. They must work alongside customers, internal and external, to promote and optimize the usage of the organization’s knowledge assets. As a Knowledge Manager, you will help define the content/knowledge management strategy. The role will focus on developing, auditing, and maintaining high quality content for client facing colleagues. Encouraging a culture of knowledge-sharing and collaborative workflow among departments and project teams Ensuring that employees are sure of the content/knowledge management processes, including the purpose of doing so, through accurately and helpful documentation of processes. Making sure that the right Content/knowledge goes to the right people, and that this information is promptly applied to business activities Helping capture new Content/knowledge with the usage of Oracle Content management (OCM) and Oracle Sales Accelerator Monitor progress and learning processes by keeping track of engagement, project outcomes and other metrics SKILLS/COMPETENCIES REQUIRED: Minimum of 1-2 years experience is desirable. title: Financial Planning Analyst - Graduate (Vikariat)
description: Financial Planning Analyst – Graduate (Vikariat) Har du for nylig afsluttet din kandidatgrad og er du en analytisk og målrettet person med interesse for finans, finansiel planlægning og analyse? Så er du måske vores nye kollega! Vi søger en ny kollega til vores Financial Planning & Analysis (FP&A)-team, som har lyst til at udvikle sine evner i en global virksomhed med en stærk kundeorienteret kultur. Din hverdag hos os Som en del af FP&A (Business Controlling) vil du spille en nøglerolle i den finansielle planlægning og analyse, der finder sted løbende i Aon. Du vil arbejde tæt sammen med ledelsen og andre kollegaer for at sikre, at vi har et solidt økonomisk fundament. Dit bidrag vil være centralt for at identificere forretningsmuligheder og optimere vores finansielle processer for at understøtte virksomhedens langsigtede mål. Dine primære opgaver vil omfatte: Udvikling og vedligeholdelse af finansielle modeller og rapportering Analysering af finansielle resultater og identificering af tendenser og afvigelser Udarbejdelse og præsentationer af finansielle rapporter til ledelsen Deltagelse i budget- og prognoseprocesser Identificering og implementering af forbedringer i finansielle processer og systemer Tæt samarbejde med andre kolleger på tværs af Aon Nordic Finance såvel som forretningen for at supportere forretningsenhederne med finansiel analyse og rådgivning Hvem leder vi efter? Vi leder efter dig, der for nyligt har afsluttet din kandidatgrad indenfor finans, erhvervsadministration eller økonomi med fokus på controlling og analyse. Du skal have stærke analytiske evner og en reel interesse i at forstå Aons forretning. Derudover leder vi efter en kandidat, som: Har godt kendskab til Excel (pivottabeller, makroer og komplekse formler) og Power BI (oprettelse og håndtering af rapporter og dashboards). Erfaring med finansielle systemer (f.eks. SAP, Oracle) er en fordel Er nysgerrig, løsningsorienteret, tør stille spørgsmål og som ikke er bange for at fejle Evner at arbejde under pres og håndtere deadlines Er entusiatisk og har en stærk arbejdsmoral og er tryg ved at arbejde alene såvel som en del af et team Tager initiativ, er proaktiv og løsningsorienteret og har sans for detaljer Er tryg ved at kommunikere med kolleger og interessenter på alle niveauer i en organisation Kommunikerer flydende på dansk og engelsk – både mundtligt og skriftligt Respekterer og forstår vigtigheden ved fortrolighedskrav Hvad vi kan tilbyde Aon er global markedsleder inden for risikorådgivning og Human Capital. Det betyder høje standarder, tydelige ambitioner og et miljø, hvor faglighed og ansvar går hånd i hånd. I denne rolle vil du få indgående og bredt kendskab til vores forskellige forretningsområder. Det er en rolle med meget ansvar, som giver stærke muligheder for faglig og personlig udvikling. Rollen er midlertidig indtil 31. oktober 2026 men med mulighed for forlængelse. Skal du være vores nye kollega? Har du spørgsmål til stillingen eller lyst til en indledende dialog, er du meget velkommen til at kontakte Nordic CFO, Charlotte Elsnitz på mail: charlotte.elsnitz@aon.com. Vi håber på at kunne ansætte en ny kollega med opstart snarest muligt, så vent ikke for længe med at uploade dit CV samt ansøgning. Vi holder løbende samtaler. Om Aon Aon plc (NYSE: AON) har til formål at udforme grundlaget for bedre beslutninger – med henblik på at beskytte og berige livet for mennesker over hele verden. Via handlingsorienteret analytisk indsigt, globalt integreret ekspertise, hvad angår risikovillig kapital og menneskelig kapital, samt løsninger, der er relevante på lokalt plan giver vores kolleger i mere end 120 lande og jurisdiktioner vores kunder den nødvendige klarhed og tillid til at træffe bedre beslutninger, hvad angår risici og medarbejdere – beslutninger, der medvirker til at beskytte og vækste deres virksomheder. I Danmark er vi 180 medarbejdere fordelt på kontorer i København, Kolding og Århus. #LI-MT1 2578915

title: Account Director
description: Account Director – Healthcare Department Are you a insurance expert seeking to advance your career? This position provides independence, responsibility for major healthcare clients, and the chance to collaborate within a dynamic, high-achieving team. Managing a portfolio of healthcare and medical clients, the Account Director handles key accounts and large nursing home insurance programs. The position reports directly to the Managing Director, National & Specialty (Commercial Risk Solutions). It focuses on identifying client risk needs and delivering personalized solutions across Aon’s capabilities. This is a hybrid role, offering flexibility to work virtually and from the Dublin office. Aon is in the business of better decisions At Aon, we shape decisions for the better to protect and enrich the lives of people around the world. As an organization, we are united through trust as one inclusive team and we are passionate about helping our colleagues and clients succeed. What the day will look like As the Account Director, you will take charge independently and assume responsibility for major healthcare clients, making certain their requirements are addressed with highly customized solutions. Your daily tasks will be diverse and stimulating, keeping you involved and motivated! Client & Portfolio Management Handle a portfolio of healthcare and medical clients with major revenue duties Act as senior client contact, building trusted, long‑term relationships Own delivery of services and service level agreements Lead renewals, placements, and negotiations with insurers Place large healthcare programmes using outstanding wordings Handle intricate claims, including significant healthcare and medical malpractice losses Attend and lead client, carrier, and market meetings Contribute to the growth of Aon Healthcare Solutions through innovation Risk Advisory & Solutions Develop a strong understanding of client businesses and risk profiles Build tailored insurance and risk solutions, including broader commercial programmes Leverage Aon’s analytics, specialty, and advisory capabilities Provide clear, practical mentorship on risk transfer and management options Business Growth & Commercial Outcomes Identify cross‑sell and upsell opportunities within existing clients Support new business activity, tenders, and pitches Maintain strong market awareness across healthcare and trade credit Collaboration & Leadership Working closely with internal teams, you will deliver a seamless client experience. You will provide mentorship and support to colleagues servicing the portfolio and have people management responsibilities including coaching, mentoring, and performance reviews. Role‑model inclusive leadership behaviours and Aon values. Governance, Compliance & Professional Standards Ensure compliance with Aon policies, regulatory requirements, and the Code of Business Conduct Act with integrity, care, and diligence at all times Advance risks or issues in line with agreed risk appetite Maintain required training and professional development Skills and experience that will lead to success Demonstrated experience in a senior, client-facing position within insurance, risk management, or financial services Strong portfolio management and renewal negotiation experience Ability to assess complex risk profiles and structure appropriate solutions Strong commercial competence, including revenue growth and retention Excellent relationship‑building and collaborator management skills Compliance with Central Bank Fitness & Probity requirements At least 5 years’ recent senior leadership experience in a commercial setting Progress towards ACII qualification (minimum MDI achieved) Confident communicator and influencer, able to adapt messages for varied internal and external audiences Proven ability to collaborate, lead, and negotiate across collaborators #LI-SS2 2576758

Client & Portfolio Management Handle a portfolio of healthcare and medical clients with major revenue duties Act as senior client contact, building trusted, long‑term relationships Own delivery of services and service level agreements Lead renewals, placements, and negotiations with insurers Place large healthcare programmes using outstanding wordings Handle intricate claims, including significant healthcare and medical malpractice losses Attend and lead client, carrier, and market meetings Contribute to the growth of Aon Healthcare Solutions through innovation Risk Advisory & Solutions Develop a strong understanding of client businesses and risk profiles Build tailored insurance and risk solutions, including broader commercial programmes Leverage Aon’s analytics, specialty, and advisory capabilities Provide clear, practical mentorship on risk transfer and management options Business Growth & Commercial Outcomes Identify cross‑sell and upsell opportunities within existing clients Support new business activity, tenders, and pitches Maintain strong market awareness across healthcare and trade credit Collaboration & Leadership Working closely with internal teams, you will deliver a seamless client experience. You will provide mentorship and support to colleagues servicing the portfolio and have people management responsibilities including coaching, mentoring, and performance reviews. Role‑model inclusive leadership behaviours and Aon values. Governance, Compliance & Professional Standards Ensure compliance with Aon policies, regulatory requirements, and the Code of Business Conduct Act with integrity, care, and diligence at all times Advance risks or issues in line with agreed risk appetite Maintain required training and professional development Skills and experience that will lead to success Demonstrated experience in a senior, client-facing position within insurance, risk management, or financial services Strong portfolio management and renewal negotiation experience Ability to assess complex risk profiles and structure appropriate solutions Strong commercial competence, including revenue growth and retention Excellent relationship‑building and collaborator management skills Compliance with Central Bank Fitness & Probity requirements At least 5 years’ recent senior leadership experience in a commercial setting Progress towards ACII qualification (minimum MDI achieved) Confident communicator and influencer, able to adapt messages for varied internal and external audiences Proven ability to collaborate, lead, and negotiate across collaborators #LI-SS2

title: Payments & Receipts Specialist
description: Life is full of risks, from unexpected hurricanes to earthquakes, terrorism to cybercrime, always ending up with financial impacts. They can't be controlled but can be managed. Join Aon to help our clients manage these risks in the world of reinsurance - the most stable financial industry, highly developed in global financial centres such as Zurich, London, Bermuda or Chicago. Payments & Receipts Specialist In this role, you’ll be part of our Prague-based team that supports over 130 insurance companies across Europe, Israel, and the Middle East. You’ll gain hands-on experience and learn all about the reinsurance industry. It’s a great opportunity to grow your skills, work with international clients, and be part of a friendly, collaborative team that’s passionate about what we do. What the day will look like Managing assigned portfolio of clients (insurance companies) and business partners (reinsurers) Ensuring on time payment performance - selection of payable transactions and issuing payments in the processing system Preparation and sending of payment notifications for all issued payments and lists of balances Monitoring of bank accounts in various currencies, monitoring of relevant due dates and chasing clients/reinsurers for settlement Cash management - responsibility for incoming payments and their allocation against respective entries in accounting system Analyses of payables and receivables & compensation possibilities Coordination of transactions on two different ledgers Written and verbal communication with insurance companies from EMEA region and reinsurers worldwide Issuing confirmation for audit purposes Cooperation with the accounting team, brokers & other internal partners Skills and experience that will lead to success 3 years of relevant experience or university degree (finance and economics) You have very good knowledge of English and French, any other EMEA language is welcomed! User knowledge of MS Office, especially Excel Experience in insurance or reinsurance business is an advantage. However, training will be in place if this is not your immediate background. Attention to detail, mathematical way of thinking and enjoy analyzing problems and implementing the appropriate solutions in a methodical manner Strong work ethic with a professional outlook, good organization skills and ability to manage stress Please note applications are available for candidates with valid work permit for Czechia. How we support our colleagues In addition to our comprehensive benefits package, we encourage an inclusive workforce. Plus, our agile environment allows you to manage your wellbeing and work/life balance, ensuring you can be your best self at Aon. Furthermore, all colleagues enjoy two “Global Wellbeing Days” each year, encouraging you to take time to focus on yourself. We offer a variety of working style solutions for our colleagues as well. Our continuous learning culture inspires and equips you to learn, share and grow, helping you achieve your fullest potential. As a result, at Aon, you are more connected, more relevant, and more valued. Aon values an innovative and inclusive workplace where all colleagues feel empowered to be their authentic selves. title: Wealth Communications Advisory Lead
description: We are seeking an experienced communications and consulting professional to lead our Wealth Communications proposition across EMEA Our Wealth Communications team designs, writes and delivers clear, engaging and impactful communications for members of both DB and DC pension schemes. We support all aspects of member engagement, from retirement planning and education campaigns to change management and strategic communication programmes. This senior role carries full accountability for delivering revenue growth, profitability, and overall financial performance for the consulting segment. You will lead the Wealth communications proposition: shaping its strategic direction, driving its development, and ensuring it delivers measurable commercial outcomes. Working in partnership with experienced stakeholders, you will also develop and lead integrated communications activity with clients that strengthens and grows our market position and deepens client engagement. Aon is in the business of better decisions At Aon, we shape decisions for the better to protect and enrich the lives of people around the world. As an organization, we are united through trust as one inclusive team and we are passionate about helping our colleagues and clients succeed. What the day will look like You will have a strong track record in business development and sales within a Wealth or related environment, with a clear understanding of client needs and effective communication strategies. You are a confident, credible communicator able to build strong relationships, influence stakeholders and collaborate across teams. Known for identifying client challenges and shaping tailored, value‑driven solutions, you are dependable, well organised and trusted to deliver on commitments while clearly articulating the value you bring to clients and prospects. Take ownership for delivering revenue growth and profitability for the Wealth Communications consulting segment in the region. Lead and deliver consulting projects ensuring they meet scope, timelines, budget and quality standards. Develop strategic communications programmes that deliver meaningful business and client outcomes Lead creative delivery – working with our design and copywriting experts to deliver high quality communications across a range of channels Identify growth opportunities within existing accounts through upselling, cross-selling, or introducing new products/services. Maintain strong client relationships and ensure high levels of satisfaction throughout delivery Manage the sales pipeline and CRM system to track opportunities and provide clear visibility for results. Accountable for exceeding the client budget target and driving client profitability contributing to the overall business financial targets. Build and maintain strong internal and external networks to increase opportunities. Work closely with the key stakeholders on all stages of the prospecting process, including pre-Request for Proposal How this opportunity is different This role supports organisations and pension schemes to communicate clearly and effectively, connecting people with the benefits, services and information that matter most to them. Through data‑driven insight, it explores what engages different workforce groups and translates this into communications that feel personal, human and relevant. By taking the uncertainty out of change, the role creates engagement strategies with people at their heart, alongside narratives that inform, reassure and guide. Clients are supported with high‑quality, consumer‑grade technology that powers these experiences – blending platforms and services into a seamless, connected journey. The role works closely with talented experts across health, wealth and talent, as well as a team of 50+ communications professionals. Skills and experience that will lead to success Proven success in Wealth communications or similar financial services engagement environments Expertise in strategic communications and multi-channel engagement Strong background in change communications Excellent writing skills and ability to collaborate with creative teams Working knowledge of design, brand and user experience principles Experience with measurement, analytics and reporting 2576935

title: Client Manager - Corporate Insurance (Chelmsford)
description: Client Manager – Corporate Insurance | Chelmsford (Hybrid) Do you thrive on building trusted relationships with Corporate clients and delivering high‑quality insurance solutions? Are you looking to develop your career with a leading global risk advisor and insurance broker? If so, this could be an excellent next step! Aon is currently recruiting a Client Manager to join our Corporate team in Chelmsford. This is a hybrid role, combining home, office and client‑based working, with a strong focus on engaging with and supporting your client portfolio as part of a collaborative client service team. Aon is in the business of better decisions At Aon, we shape decisions for the better to protect and enrich the lives of people around the world. As an organization, we are united through trust as one inclusive team and we are passionate about helping our colleagues and clients succeed. What the day will look like The Client Manager leads a portfolio of our key clients, handling the relationship, ensuring excellent service provision for the client through their dedicated service team and Aon’s specialist resources, and ensuring they are aware of the full capability of Aon to grow successful long-term client partnerships. Corporate clients typically require bespoke insurance programmes and associated risk management and risk transfer services. Besides being the insurance broker, the Corporate team in Chelmsford provides clients advice on insurance and risk management across most industries, with specialisms in food and beverage; retail; hospitality and digital economy. Key responsibilities include: Lead the service provided to clients by our Corporate Team. Ensure the highest level of service to each assigned client in line with the agreed client service tools. Identify and introduce Aon’s products and services to meet the client’s needs and understand and communicate each Client’s business issues and needs to the service team and other parts of Aon. Build client relationships with key decision-makers at the client. Grow the revenue and profitability of your client portfolio, including meeting annual revenue target for this portfolio. Responsible for working in accordance with the Aon UK Limited Risk Management Framework, and compliance with the Aon UK Limited policies, including participation in the management of risks (including completion of mandatory training) that may adversely affect the business, interests or reputation of any Group Company. How this opportunity is different This is a great opportunity to work build a career in a team with a proven track record of developing talent. Working within a business with a huge amount of innovate solutions to offer to client. You will be supported by an experienced leadership team who want to see you succeed. Skills and experience that will lead to success Proven experience in the insurance industry, with experience in client relationship management and the core lines of commercial risks is essential. Experience handling large, corporate insurance risks is desirable. Commercial understanding of business i.e. market forces, business drivers, market dynamics, competitors and risks, etc. Prior experience with sales, fee setting, negotiation and influencing. An effective communicator and presenter. #LI-JV2 #LI-HYBRID 2577889

Client Manager, Global & Corporate A core Client Segment within Aon Risk Solutions, Corporate & Global specializes in the provision of exceptional Insurance and Risk Management Services to a focused portfolio of national and multinational organisations. We are seeking an experienced and skilled Client Manager to provide exceptional broking service a portfolio of clients. The Client Manager will manage complex insurance programs and provide first class client service. An important responsibility is also growing and developing the broader team. While the action happens in the Aon Adelaide office, there is opportunity for hybrid working. Aon is in the business of better decisions At Aon, we shape decisions for the better to protect and enrich the lives of people around the world. As an organization, we are united through trust as one inclusive, diverse team, and we are passionate about helping our colleagues and clients succeed. The Client Manager has a key role to play in contributing to the strategic direction of the South Australian Global & Corporate business. Here at Aon, you’re always more than just a job title. We encourage and support you to expand your knowledge and grow alongside some of the best people in the business – in Australia and throughout our connected global networks. title: Gerente Comercial Consultivo - Broker - Seguros
description: Gerente Comercial / Consultivo - Soluciones de Seguros ¿Te gustaría unirte a un equipo diverso e inclusivo? ¿Buscas impactar con tu conocimiento una empresa trasnacional líder? ¡Esta oportunidad está esperando por ti! Aon está en el negocio de las mejores decisiones. En Aon, damos forma a las decisiones para mejoría para proteger y enriquecer las vidas de las personas alrededor del mundo. Como organización, estamos unidos a través de la confianza como un equipo inclusivo y diverso, y nos apasiona ayudar a nuestros colegas y clientes a tener éxito. Cómo será el día típico. Buscamos un profesional orientado a la venta consultiva de soluciones aseguradoras para clientes empresariales (B2B), capaz de comprender el negocio y los riesgos de cada industria (energética, construcción, financiera, manufactura, etc.), traduciendo estos riesgos en propuestas personalizadas alineadas con los objetivos financieros y contractuales de cada cliente. Este perfil combina habilidades técnicas del sector asegurador con competencias consultivas, estratégicas y relacionales, enfocadas en resultados y sustentadas en una gestión disciplinada del proceso comercial. Competencias Técnicas y Estratégicas necesarias para el puesto: Conocimiento del sector asegurador y del cliente B2B: Dominio de productos multiramo (responsabilidad civil, construcción, crédito, salud, etc) Experiencia en el sector Asegurador, Rámos Técnicos, Consultorias de AAA, Head Hunter Servicios Financieros Gestión estratégica de cuentas clave Venta consultiva y asesoría empresarial Desarrollo de Negocios Large y Medium Habilidades y experiencia que conducirán al éxito. Experiencia comprobable en venta consultiva B2B, preferentemente en seguros o servicios financieros. Inglés Fluido Perfil Profesional de Negocios Licenciatura o Ingeniería concluida, 100% créditos Capacidad para realizar prospección en frío y generar pipeline multicanal, diferenciando mensajes por industria. Habilidad para identificar riesgos operativos, contractuales y financieros, y traducirlos en soluciones aseguradoras. Manejo disciplinado de CRM y análisis de datos para priorizar industrias y cuentas con mayor potencial de conversión/rentabilidad. Inglés Intermedio Cómo apoyamos a nuestros compañeros Además de nuestro paquete integral de beneficios, fomentamos una fuerza laboral diversa. Además, nuestro entorno ágil e inclusivo te permite gestionar tu bienestar y el equilibrio entre el trabajo y la vida personal, lo que garantiza que puedas dar lo mejor de ti mismo en Aon. Además, todos los compañeros disfrutan de dos “Global Wellbeing Days” cada año, lo que los anima a tomarse un tiempo para centrarse en sí mismos. Ofrecemos una variedad de soluciones de estilo de trabajo, pero también reconocemos que la flexibilidad va más allá del lugar de trabajo... Y todos estamos a favor. ¡A esto lo llamamos Smart Working! Nuestra cultura de aprendizaje continuo te inspira y te equipa para aprender, compartir y crecer, ayudándote a alcanzar tu máximo potencial. Como resultado, en Aon, estás más conectado, eres más relevante y valorado. Aon valora un lugar de trabajo innovador y diverso en el que todos los compañeros se sientan capacitados para ser ellos mismos. Aon se enorgullece de ser un lugar de trabajo que ofrece igualdad de oportunidades. Aon ofrece igualdad de oportunidades de empleo a todos los empleados y solicitantes de empleo sin distinción de raza, color, religión, credo, sexo, orientación sexual, identidad de género, origen nacional, edad, discapacidad, veterano, estado civil, pareja de hecho u otro estado legalmente protegido. Damos la bienvenida a las solicitudes de todos y proporcionamos a las personas con discapacidades ajustes razonables para participar en la solicitud de empleo, el proceso de entrevista y para realizar las funciones esenciales del trabajo una vez a bordo. 2572744
